text:
Floor Masters Due to the way that the group is referred in the Japanese text and Viz translation, their title itself ("Floor Masters" in the plural) never appears; instead, the expression "a Floor Master" and other textual constructions are used when referring to them. In the Crunchyroll's translation of the 2011 anime adaptation, the term "Floor Masters" is used even though it does not exactly match the characters' speech lines. (フロアマスター, Furoa Masutā) are 21 elite fighters in Heavens Arena. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 49 The only known canon Floor Masters are Chrollo Lucilfer Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 351 and Hisoka Morow. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 

Overview

Floor Masters are the 21 highest ranked competitors in Heavens Arena. They have that title because they're given their own floor between the 230 th and 250 th floors. 

Qualifications

In order to become a Floor Master, one must first win 10 matches on the 200 th floor of the celestial tower, and must not lose 4 matches. Then they must challenge a Floor Master to take his or her floor. 

Benefits

Being a Floor Master means having one's own floor. A Floor Master can also participate in Battle Olympia, a biennial tournament where the victor is allowed to live on the 251 st floor in an exclusive penthouse. 

Non-Canon Floor Masters

Amoeba

Trivia

Until Chapter 351, all the known Floor Masters appeared only in the movie Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 
Hisoka became eligible to challenge a Floor Master after defeating Gon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 63 
Kastro was deemed as strong as a Floor Master, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 52 but he never managed to challenge one due to his death at the hands of Hisoka.

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, an image with several of them is shown while a Heavens Arena attendant explains what a Floor Master is. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 39 (1999) 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, the Floor Masters are only mentioned. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 29 (2011)
